item.title: Ardian Plans to Invest Over €3 Billion in Nordic Data Centers
item.description: Ardian SAS plans to invest over €3 billion ($3.4 billion) to build data centers in the Nordics, as lower energy prices and ample available land draw more digital infrastructure providers to the region.

item.title: Citadel Securities Warns of ‘Shifting Landscape’ Under Warsh Fed
item.description: Investors are underestimating how determined Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is to bring inflation back to the central bank’s 2% target and the likely drag that will create on risk assets, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: A Potentially Terrible AI Economic Dilemma
item.description: Austerity for the non-AI economy?

item.title: Bomb Attack Damages Ecuador Mining Agency Probing Illegal Gold
item.description: A bomb apparently targeting Ecuador’s mining regulator blew out several floors of windows at a major government building in the capital of Quito early Monday morning.

item.title: Iraq’s 14 Million Barrels of Trapped Oil Escape Through Hormuz
item.description: Iraqi oil stranded by the Middle East conflict has escaped the Persian Gulf over the past 10 days as transit via the Strait of Hormuz eased during an uneasy US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Supreme Court Thwarts Trump Bid to Oust Fed’s Cook for Now
item.description: The US Supreme Court ruled that Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook can stay in her job for now, reinforcing the central bank’s independence from the White House and dealing a setback to President Donald Trump.

item.title: Mexico Exchange Halts Trading Ahead of Session Open
item.description: Mexico’s BMV Group halted trading at the country’s main exchange before the start of the Monday session.

item.title: Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
item.description: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Private Credit Fund Kicks Off Debt Offering
item.description: A Morgan Stanley private credit fund is selling investment-grade bonds to refinance debt, its first offering in just over a year.

item.title: Kenya, Congo Eurobonds Among Top Winners From Iran War Trade Unwind
item.description: Kenya and Democratic Republic of Congo are among the top performing African debt markets this month, as tumbling crude prices lure investors back to securities issued by oil importers.

item.title: Europe’s Boldest Tech Startups Are Reaching for US SPACs Again
item.description: Newcleo Ltd., a European startup building novel nuclear reactors that run on recycled waste, has the potential to disrupt clean energy. As with all new technology, the path is not easy.

item.title: Poor Pod Counts Signal Sharp Drop in Ivory Coast Cocoa Crop
item.description: The cocoa crop in top grower Ivory Coast could fall by about a fifth in the next season as the number of pods on trees lags, risking tighter global supplies, according to traders surveyed by Bloomberg.

item.title: Investors Snub a 1,400 Basis-Point Outperformance in Small Caps
item.description: The smallest, riskiest group of US stocks has put in a magnificent performance in the first half of the year. You never would have guessed it by looking at investor positioning.

item.title: US Futures Up as Dip Buyers Lift Tech, US-Iran Hostilities Fade
item.description: Dip buyers emerging to pick up beaten-down technology stocks and easing tensions in the Middle East helped lift US stock-index futures before the bell on Monday.

item.title: Relx Plans to Buy Back Up to £100 Million in Shares Next Month
item.description: Relx Plc plans to repurchase shares valued at as much as £100 million ($132.1 million) next month, as the British information and analytics company continues to grapple with concerns that artificial intelligence tools will displace its core research products.

item.title: SpaceX Pushes US Share Sales to Record $251 Billion at Midyear
item.description: Wall Street bankers are on a high after record-setting offerings from SpaceX and Google parent Alphabet Inc., lifting expectations for deal activity in the rest of 2026.

item.title: Eni and Adnoc Investment Arm XRG Buy into Argentine LNG Project
item.description: Italian energy giant Eni SpA and the investment unit of the Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. agreed to buy stakes in a liquefied natural gas production and export project in Argentina.

item.title: Strategy Says It May Sell Up to $1.25 Billion of Bitcoin
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. unveiled a sweeping overhaul of the financing model underpinning its Bitcoin strategy, giving itself broader powers to sell the cryptocurrency, buy back securities and preserve liquidity as it adapts to mounting pressure on the structure that fueled years of aggressive accumulation.

item.title: Millennium Builds AI Lab in Push for Cutting-Edge Products
item.description: Millennium Management is setting up an artificial intelligence laboratory to expand the development and application of cutting-edge technologies at the firm.

item.title: Suez Wins Its Largest Mideast Deal With €2 Billion Oman Contract
item.description: Suez SA and partners won a €2 billion ($2.3 billion) water-services contract in Oman, the French firm’s largest deal in the Middle East.

item.title: The Uninvited Meeting Guest Is an AI Notetaker
item.description: AI bots are showing up to remote meetings faster than humans can create social norms to guide them.

item.title: Hormuz Recovery Hits a Bumpy Patch After Fresh Attacks on Ships
item.description: The reopening of the crucial waterway faltered after Iran struck two ships.

item.title: Microsoft’s $570 Billion Rout Sets Up Its Worst Month Since 2000
item.description: Microsoft Corp. shares are heading for their worst month since the dot-com era as investors continue to fret about how the software giant will fare in a world marked by artificial intelligence.

item.title: UK Growth Outlook Brightens After US-Iran Truce Lowers Oil Costs
item.description: The fall in global oil prices is “unequivocally good news” for Britain’s economy, Bloomberg Economics said as it lifted growth forecasts for this year.

item.title: Mercuria Signs First Uranium Financing Deal With Malawi Miner
item.description: Trading house Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed its first prepayment agreement with a uranium miner, striking a deal with the owner of an operation in Malawi.

item.title: Burnham Vows Economic Boost by Taking Fiscal Power Out of London
item.description: Andy Burnham has pledged to radically devolve fiscal powers away from Westminster should he succeed in his bid to replace Keir Starmer as prime minister.

item.title: Warsh Joins ECB’s Soul Searching in Sintra
item.description: The ECB’s annual forum will puzzle over feeble growth and ponder the dangers of AI

item.title: Ferrari Luce, New Golf Cart Reflect Apple Design Aesthetic
item.description: Ive, Hoenig reflect their Apple experience in new vehicles

item.title: Global Trade Braces for Another Period of Policy Uncertainty
item.description: Ties between China and the EU are fraying, while the North American free trade deal looks set to undergo reviews

item.title: UK Political Angst Turned Foreigners Into Gilt Sellers in May
item.description: Foreign investors, long an anchor for the UK government bond market, turned net sellers of gilts in May as climbing yields failed to offset concern about the country’s political turbulence.
